answer:
**The *Viṣṇu Smṛti* (Chapter 43) enumerates a sequence of 22 hells (*narakas*), beginning with Tāmisra and Andhatāmisra, passing through Raurava, Mahāraurava, Kālasūtra, Saṃjīvana, Avīci, Kuḍmala, Pūtimṛttika, Lohaśaṅku, Kaṇṭakaśālmali, and Asipatravana, and ending with Lohacāraka (among others listed in between).**[^1]

The named hells matching the query are quoted below (in chapter order, with verse markers):

> Tāmisra (Darkness). // Vi_43.2 //  
> Andhatāmisra (Blinding Darkness). // Vi_43.3 //  
> Raurava (Howling). // Vi_43.4 //  
> Mahāraurava (Great Howling). // Vi_43.5 //  
> Kālasūtra (The Black Thread). // Vi_43.6 //  
> Saṃjīvana (Reviving). // Vi_43.8 //  
> Avīci (Waveless). // Vi_43.9 //  
> Kuḍmala (Bud). // Vi_43.14 //  
> Pūtimṛttika (Putrid Clay). // Vi_43.15 //  
> Lohaśaṅku (Iron Spike). // Vi_43.16 //  
> Kaṇṭakaśālmali (Thorny Silk-Cotton Tree). // Vi_43.19 //  
> Asipatravana (Forest of Sword-leaves). // Vi_43.21 //  
> Lohacāraka (Iron Cage). // Vi_43.22 //[^1]

The duration verses (immediately following the list) state:

> In these, those who have committed heinous sins without performing *prāyaścitta* are tormented in succession for a Kalpa. // Vi_43.23 //  
> Great sinners, for a Manvantara. // Vi_43.24 //[^2]

[^1]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 43 (paragraphs p-c760bd through p-7f9b8e)](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/43/translation/#p-c760bd).
[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 43 (paragraphs p-fc7b11 and p-cd0885)](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/43/translation/#p-fc7b11).
